title: microstep bound (100k default, config)
manager: A hard ceiling on how many microsteps a single macrostep may take — 100k by default, configurable — so a machine that would otherwise spin forever in eventless transitions faults loudly instead of hanging the host process. This is FSL's totality backstop: it turns "this might not terminate" from an undecidable worry into a bounded, observable error you can tune per machine, which is the pragmatic price of letting the graph loop at all.
cs: microstep counter with a configurable bound (default 100k); exceeding it raises a fault at the current stable point; the guardrail behind the eventless/else cascade (W6.90) and the RTC internal-queue drain (W6.73).
